name: bugsnag

metadata.name: bugsnag
metadata.summary: Automatic error monitoring for django, flask, etc.

documentation:
Bugsnag
=======

The official Python notifier for `Bugsnag <https://bugsnag.com/>`_.
Provides support for automatically capturing and sending exceptions
in your Django and other Python apps to Bugsnag, to help you find
and solve your bugs as fast as possible.

Connect the bugsnag failure_handler to the Celery
task_failure signal

classes.name: bugsnag.wsgi.middleware.BugsnagMiddleware
documentation:
Notifies Bugsnag on any unhandled exception that happens while processing
a request in your application.

This middleware should be installed before any middlewares that catch
the exception and render an error messae.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.asynchronous
documentation: If API requests should be sent asynchronously

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.auto_capture_sessions
documentation:
If sessions should be automatically detected and delivered from web
request integrations

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.auto_notify
documentation: If uncaught exceptions should be automatically captured and reported

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.delivery
documentation:
Transport mechanism used to make API requests. Implement the Delivery
interface to customize how requests are sent.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.endpoint
documentation:
Event API endpoint. Set this property if using Bugsnag On-Premise.

```pycon
>>> config = Configuration()
>>> config.endpoint = 'https://notify.bugsnag.example.co'
```

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.hostname
documentation:
The host name of the application server. This value is automatically
detected for Heroku applications and included in event device metadata.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.ignore_classes
documentation:
Fully qualified class names which should be ignored when capturing
uncaught exceptions and other events. KeyboardInterrupt and Http404
exceptions are ignored by default.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.lib_root
documentation:
The path to the Python library. Any traceback frame which contains
lib_root as a prefix is considered out-of-project. The prefix is also
stripped to make file names easier to read.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.notify_release_stages
documentation:
A list of release_stage values which are permitted to capture and send
events and sessions. By default this value is None and all events and
sessions are delivered.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.params_filters
documentation:
A list of filters applied to event metadata to prevent the values from
being sent in events. By default the following keys are filtered:

* authorization
* cookie
* password
* password_confirmation

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.project_root
documentation:
The working directory containing the application source code.
Traceback file paths which contain this prefix are considered a part of
the project. This prefix is also stripped to increase file name
readability in traceback lines.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.proxy_host
documentation: The host name of the proxy to use to deliver requests, if any

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.release_stage
documentation:
The development phase of the deployed application. This value is used
to differentiate events which occur in production vs development or
staging environments.

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.send_code
documentation:
If the source code lines immediately surrounding traceback locations
should be sent with events

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.send_environment
documentation:
If the request environment should be automatically collected and
attached to events

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.session_endpoint
documentation:
Sessions API endpoint. Set this property if using Bugsnag On-Premise.

```pycon
>>> config = Configuration()
>>> config.session_endpoint = 'https://sessions.bugsnag.example.co'
```

methods.name: bugsnag.configuration.Configuration.traceback_exclude_modules
documentation: Modules which should be stripped from event tracebacks entirely

documentation: Logger for use internally

classes.name: bugsnag.asgi.BugsnagMiddleware
documentation:
Sends unhandled exceptions to Bugsnag which happen while processing
requests.

```pycon
>>> async def app(scope, receive, send):
...     await send({
...         "type": "http.request",
...         "body": b"Hello World",
...         "more_body": False,
...     })
>>> app = BugsnagMiddleware(app)
```

functions.name: bugsnag.utils.parse_content_type
documentation:
Generate a tuple of (type, subtype, suffix, parameters) from a type based
on RFC 6838

```pycon
>>> parse_content_type("text/plain")
('text', 'plain', None, None)
>>> parse_content_type("application/hal+json")
('application', 'hal', 'json', None)
>>> parse_content_type("application/json;schema=\"ftp://example.com/a\"")
('application', 'json', None, 'schema="ftp://example.com/a"')
```

functions.name: bugsnag.utils.is_json_content_type
documentation:
Check if a content type is JSON-parseable

```pycon
>>> is_json_content_type('text/plain')
False
>>> is_json_content_type('application/schema+json')
True
>>> is_json_content_type('application/json')
True
```

functions.name: bugsnag.utils._validate_setter
documentation:
Check that the first argument of a function is of a provided set of types
before calling the body of the wrapped function, printing a runtime warning
(or raising a TypeError) if the validation fails.

classes.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder

methods.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder.filter_string_values
documentation: Remove any value from the dictionary which match the key filters

methods.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder.default
documentation:
Coerce values to strings if possible, otherwise replace with
'[BADENCODING]'

methods.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder._sanitize
documentation:
Replace recursive values and trim strings longer than
MAX_STRING_LENGTH

methods.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder._sanitize_dict_key_value
documentation:
Safely sets the provided key on the dictionary by coercing the key
to a string

methods.name: bugsnag.utils.SanitizingJSONEncoder._sanitize_dict
documentation:
Trim individual values in an object, applying filtering if the object
is a FilterDict

A JSON encoder which handles filtering and conversion from JSON-
incompatible types to strings.

```pycon
>>> import logging
>>> from json import loads
>>> logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
>>> encoder = SanitizingJSONEncoder(logger, keyword_filters=['bananas'])
>>> items = loads(encoder.encode(FilterDict({'carrots': 4, 'bananas': 5})))
>>> items['bananas']
'[FILTERED]'
>>> items['carrots']
4
```

classes.name: bugsnag.client.Client

methods.name: bugsnag.client.Client.capture
documentation:
Run a block of code within the clients context.
Any exception raised will be reported to bugsnag.

```pycon
>>> with client.capture():  # doctest: +SKIP
...     raise Exception('an exception passed to bugsnag then reraised')
```

The context can optionally include specific types to capture.

```pycon
>>> with client.capture((TypeError,)):  # doctest: +SKIP
...     raise Exception('an exception which does get captured')
```

Alternately, functions can be decorated to capture any
exceptions thrown during execution and reraised.

```pycon
>>> @client.capture  # doctest: +SKIP
... def foo():
...     raise Exception('an exception passed to bugsnag then reraised')
```

The decoration can optionally include specific types to capture.

```pycon
>>> @client.capture((TypeError,))  # doctest: +SKIP
... def foo():
...     raise Exception('an exception which does not get captured')
```

methods.name: bugsnag.client.Client.notify
documentation:
Notify bugsnag of an exception.

```pycon
>>> client.notify(Exception('Example'))  # doctest: +SKIP
```

methods.name: bugsnag.client.Client.notify_exc_info
documentation:
Notify bugsnag of an exception via exc_info.

```pycon
>>> client.notify_exc_info(*sys.exc_info())  # doctest: +SKIP
```

methods.name: bugsnag.client.Client.deliver
documentation: Deliver the exception event to Bugsnag.

A Bugsnag monitoring and reporting client.

```pycon
>>> client = Client(api_key='...')  # doctest: +SKIP
```

functions.name: bugsnag.tornado.tornado_environ
documentation:
Copyright The Tornado Web Library Authors

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

Converts a tornado request to a WSGI environment.

Taken from tornado's WSGI implementation
https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/6e3521da44c349197cf8048c8a6c69d3f4ccd971/tornado/wsgi.py#L207-L246
but without WSGI prefixed entries that require a WSGI application.

classes.name: bugsnag.event.Event

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event.__init__
documentation:
Create a new event

exception is the exception being reported.
config is the global instance of bugsnag.Configuration
request_config is the thread-local instance of bugsnag.Configuration
(used by middleware)

options can be used to override any of the configuration parameters:
    "api_key", "release_stage", "app_version", "hostname"
and to provide the following top-level event payload keys:
    "user", "context", "severity", "grouping_hash", "metadata",
    ("user_id")
or to provide the exception parameter:
    "traceback"
All other keys will be sent as metadata to Bugsnag.

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event.set_user
documentation: Set user parameters on event.

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event.add_custom_data
documentation: Add data to the "custom" metadata tab

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event.add_tab
documentation:
Add a metadata tab to the event

If the tab already exists, the new content will be merged into the
existing content.

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event._generate_stacktrace
documentation: Build the stacktrace

methods.name: bugsnag.event.Event._code_for
documentation: Find the code around this line in the file.
An occurrence of an exception for delivery to Bugsnag

methods.name: bugsnag.request_tracker.RequestTracker.new_request
documentation:
Track a new request, returning a callback that marks the request as
complete.

```pycon
>>> request_tracker = RequestTracker()
>>> mark_request_complete = request_tracker.new_request()
>>> # ...make the request...
>>> mark_request_complete()
```

methods.name: bugsnag.request_tracker.RequestTracker.has_in_flight_requests
documentation:
See if there are any requests that have not been marked as completed.

```pycon
>>> request_tracker = RequestTracker()
>>> request_tracker.has_in_flight_requests()
False
>>> mark_request_complete = request_tracker.new_request()
>>> request_tracker.has_in_flight_requests()
True
>>> mark_request_complete()
>>> request_tracker.has_in_flight_requests()
False
```

functions.name: bugsnag.middleware.skip_bugsnag_middleware
documentation:
A callback-based middleware that prevents notifying an event where the
'original_error' has a 'skip_bugsnag' attr set to 'True'.

classes.name: bugsnag.middleware.DefaultMiddleware
documentation:
DefaultMiddleware provides the transformation from request_config into
metadata that has always been supported by bugsnag-python.

classes.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.before_notify
documentation:
Add a function to be run before bugsnag is notified.

This lets you modify the payload that will be sent.
If your function returns False, nothing will be sent.

```pycon
>>> def add_request_data(event):
...    event.add_tab("request", request_data)
>>>
>>> stack = MiddlewareStack()
>>> stack.before_notify(add_request_data)
```

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.after_notify
documentation:
Add a function to be run after bugsnag is notified.

This lets you log errors in custom ways.

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.append
documentation:
Add a middleware to the end of the stack.

It will be run after all middleware currently defined.
If you want to stop the event progress, return from
your \_\_call\_\_ method without calling the next level.

```pycon
>>> class ExampleMiddleware():
...     def __init__(self, bugsnag):
...         self.bugsnag = bugsnag
...     def __call__(self, event):
...         config = event.request_config
...         event.add_tab("request", config.get("request"))
...         self.bugsnag(event)
...
>>> stack = MiddlewareStack()
>>> stack.append(ExampleMiddleware)
```

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.insert_before
documentation:
Adds a middleware to the stack in the position before
the target_class.

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.insert_after
documentation:
Adds a middleware to the stack in the position after
the target_class.

methods.name: bugsnag.middleware.MiddlewareStack.run
documentation: Run all the middleware in order, then call the callback.
Manages a stack of Bugsnag middleware.
